check_class.c

00008  * 2 only, as published by the Free Software Foundation.

00011  * WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of
00012  * MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU
00013  * General Public License version 2 for more details (a copy is
00014  * included at /legal/license.txt).

00017  * version 2 along with this work; if not, write to the Free Software
00018  * Foundation, Inc., 51 Franklin St, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA
00019  * 02110-1301 USA

00022  * Clara, CA 95054 or visit www.sun.com if you need additional
00023  * information or have any questions.
00024  */
00025 
00026 /*=========================================================================
00027  * SYSTEM:    Verifier
00028  * SUBSYSTEM: Verifies ClassClass structure.
00029  * FILE:      check_class.c
00030  * OVERVIEW:  Code for verifying the ClassClass structure for internal
00031  *            consistency.
00032  *            Initial implementation based on the Classic VM Verifier.
00033  *=======================================================================*/
00034 
00035 /*=========================================================================
00036  * Include files
00037  *=======================================================================*/
00038 
00039 #include "wrappers.h"
00040 
00041 #include "oobj.h"
00042 #include "utf.h"
00043 #include "tree.h"
00044 #include "sys_api.h"
00045 
00046 /*=========================================================================
00047  * Globals and extern declarations
00048  *=======================================================================*/
00049 
00050 extern bool verify_class_codes(ClassClass *cb);
00051 
00052 static bool verify_constant_pool(ClassClass *cb);
00053 
00054 static bool is_legal_fieldname(ClassClass *cb, char *name, int type);
00055 static bool is_legal_method_signature(ClassClass *cb, char *name, char *signature);
00056 static bool is_legal_field_signature(ClassClass *cb, char *name, char *signature);
00057 
00058 static char *skip_over_fieldname(char *name, bool slash_okay);
00059 static char *skip_over_field_signature(char *name, bool void_okay);
00060 
00061 static void CCerror (ClassClass *cb, char *format, ...);
00062 
00063 /* Argument for is_legal_fieldname */
00064 enum { LegalClass, LegalField, LegalMethod };
00065 
00066 /*=========================================================================
00067  * FUNCTION:      VerifyClass
00068  * OVERVIEW:      Verifies a class given a pointer to the ClassClass struct.
00069  *                Returns true if the class is ok.
00070  * INTERFACE:
00071  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00072  *
00073  *   returns:     boolean type
00074  *=======================================================================*/
00075 bool
00076 VerifyClass(ClassClass *cb)
00077 {
00078     bool result = true;
00079     struct methodblock *mb;
00080     struct fieldblock *fb;
00081     int i;
00082     if (!verify_constant_pool(cb))
00083         return false;
00084     /* Make sure all the method names and signatures are okay */
00085     for (i = cbMethodsCount(cb), mb = cbMethods(cb); --i >= 0; mb++) {
00086     char *name = mb->fb.name;
00087     char *signature = mb->fb.signature;
00088     if (! (is_legal_fieldname(cb, name, LegalMethod)  &&
00089            is_legal_method_signature(cb, name, signature)))
00090         result = false;
00091     }
00092     /* Make sure all the field names and signatures are okay */
00093     for (i = cbFieldsCount(cb), fb = cbFields(cb); --i >= 0; fb++) {
00094     if (!  (is_legal_fieldname(cb, fb->name, LegalField) &&
00095         is_legal_field_signature(cb, fb->name, fb->signature)))
00096         result = false;
00097     }
00098     /* Make sure we are not overriding any final methods or classes*/
00099     if (cbIsInterface(cb)) {
00100     struct methodblock *mb;
00101     if ((cbSuperclass(cb) == NULL) ||
00102         (cbSuperclass(cb) != classJavaLangObject)) {
00103         CCerror(cb, "Interface %s has bad superclass", cbName(cb));
00104         result = false;
00105     }
00106     for (i = cbMethodsCount(cb), mb = cbMethods(cb); --i >= 0; mb++) {
00107         if (mb->fb.access & ACC_STATIC) {
00108         if (mb->fb.name[0] != '<') {
00109             /* Only internal methods can be static */
00110             CCerror(cb, "Illegal static method %s in interface %s",
00111                 mb->fb.name, cbName(cb));
00112             result = false;
00113         }
00114         }
00115     }
00116     } else if (cbSuperclass(cb)) {
00117     ClassClass *super_cb;
00118     unsigned bitvector_size = (unsigned)(cbMethodTableSize(cb) + 31) >> 5;
00119     long *bitvector = sysCalloc(bitvector_size, sizeof(long));
00120     for (super_cb = cbSuperclass(cb); ; super_cb = cbSuperclass(super_cb)) {
00121         if (cbAccess(super_cb) & ACC_FINAL) {
00122         CCerror(cb, "Class %s is subclass of final class %s",
00123             cbName(cb), cbName(super_cb));
00124         result = false;
00125         }
00126         mb = cbMethods(super_cb);
00127         for (i = cbMethodsCount(super_cb); --i >= 0; mb++) {
00128         if (mb->fb.access & ACC_FINAL) {
00129             unsigned offset = mb->fb.u.offset;
00130             bitvector[offset >> 5] |= (1 << (offset & 0x1F));
00131         }
00132         }
00133         if (cbSuperclass(super_cb) == NULL) break;
00134     }
00135     for (i = cbMethodsCount(cb), mb = cbMethods(cb); --i >= 0; mb++) {
00136         unsigned offset = mb->fb.u.offset;
00137         if ((offset > 0)
00138            && bitvector[offset >> 5] & (1 << (offset & 0x1F))) {
00139         CCerror(cb, "Class %s overrides final method %s.%s",
00140             cbName(cb), mb->fb.name, mb->fb.signature);
00141         result = false;
00142         }
00143     }
00144     sysFree(bitvector);
00145     } else if (cb != classJavaLangObject) {
00146     CCerror(cb, "Class %s does not have superclass", cbName(cb));
00147     result = false;
00148     }
00149 
00150     if (result)
00151     result = verify_class_codes(cb);
00152     return result;
00153 }
00154 
00155 /*=========================================================================
00156  * FUNCTION:      verify_constant_pool
00157  * OVERVIEW:      Verifies the constant pool given a pointer to the
00158  *                ClassClass structure.
00159  *                Makes two quick passes over the constant pool. The first
00160  *                pass ensures that everything is of the right type.
00161  *                Returns true if the constant pool is ok.
00162  * INTERFACE:
00163  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00164  *
00165  *   returns:     boolean type
00166  *=======================================================================*/
00167 static bool
00168 verify_constant_pool(ClassClass *cb)
00169 {
00170     union cp_item_type *cp = cbConstantPool(cb);
00171     long cp_count = cbConstantPoolCount(cb);
00172     unsigned char *type_table;
00173     int i, type;
00174 
00175     const int utf8_resolved = (CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED);
00176 
00177     if (cp_count == 0) /* Primitive classes */
00178         return true;
00179     type_table = cp[CONSTANT_POOL_TYPE_TABLE_INDEX].type;
00180     /* Let's make two quick passes over the constant pool. The first one
00181      * checks that everything is of the right type.            */
00182     for (i = 1; i < cp_count; i++) {
00183     switch(type = type_table[i]) {
00184         case CONSTANT_String:
00185         case CONSTANT_Class: {
00186         int index = cp[i].i;
00187         if (   (index < 1)
00188                || (index >= cp_count)
00189                || (type_table[index] != utf8_resolved)) {
00190             CCerror(cb, "Bad index in constant pool #%d", i);
00191             return false;
00192         }
00193         break;
00194         }
00195 
00196         case CONSTANT_String | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00197         /* This can only happen if a string is the "initial" value of
00198          * some final static String.  We assume that the checking has
00199          * already been done.
00200          */
00201         break;
00202 
00203         case CONSTANT_Fieldref:
00204         case CONSTANT_Methodref:
00205         case CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref:
00206         case CONSTANT_NameAndType: {
00207         unsigned index = (unsigned)(cp[i].i);
00208         int key1 = index >> 16;
00209         int key2 = index & 0xFFFF;
00210         if (key1 < 1 || key1 >= cp_count
00211               || key2 < 1 || key2 >= cp_count) {
00212             CCerror(cb, "Bad index in constant pool #%d", i);
00213             return false;
00214         }
00215         if (type == CONSTANT_NameAndType) {
00216             if (   (type_table[key1] != utf8_resolved)
00217             || (type_table[key2] != utf8_resolved)) {
00218             CCerror(cb, "Bad index in constant pool.");
00219             return false;
00220             }
00221         } else {
00222             if (     ((type_table[key1] & CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_TYPEMASK)
00223                     != CONSTANT_Class)
00224               || ((type_table[key2] != CONSTANT_NameAndType))) {
00225             CCerror(cb, "Bad index in constant pool #%d", i);
00226             return false;
00227             }
00228         }
00229         break;
00230         }
00231 
00232         case CONSTANT_Fieldref | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00233         case CONSTANT_Methodref | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00234         case CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00235         case CONSTANT_NameAndType | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00236             CCerror(cb, "Improperly resolved constant pool #%d", i);
00237             return false;
00238 
00239         case CONSTANT_Class | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00240         case CONSTANT_Utf8 | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00241         case CONSTANT_Integer | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00242         case CONSTANT_Float | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00243         break;
00244 
00245         case CONSTANT_Long | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00246         case CONSTANT_Double | CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED:
00247         if ((i + 1 >= cp_count) ||
00248             (type_table[i + 1] != CONSTANT_POOL_ENTRY_RESOLVED)) {
00249             CCerror(cb, "Improper constant pool long/double #%d", i);
00250             return false;
00251         } else {
00252             i++;
00253             break;
00254         }
00255 
00256         case CONSTANT_Integer:
00257         case CONSTANT_Float:
00258         case CONSTANT_Long:
00259         case CONSTANT_Double:
00260         case CONSTANT_Utf8:
00261             CCerror(cb, "Improperly unresolved constant pool #%d", i);
00262             return false;
00263 
00264         default:
00265             CCerror(cb, "Illegal constant pool type at #%d", i);
00266             return false;
00267 
00268     }
00269     }
00270     for (i = 1; i < cp_count; i++) {
00271     switch(type = type_table[i]) {
00272         case CONSTANT_Class: {
00273         int index = cp[i].i;
00274         if (!is_legal_fieldname(cb, cp[index].cp, LegalClass))
00275             return false;
00276         break;
00277         }
00278 
00279         case CONSTANT_Fieldref:
00280         case CONSTANT_Methodref:
00281         case CONSTANT_InterfaceMethodref: {
00282         unsigned index = (unsigned)(cp[i].i);
00283         int name_type_index = index & 0xFFFF;
00284         int name_type_key = cp[name_type_index].i;
00285         int name_index = name_type_key >> 16;
00286         int signature_index = name_type_key & 0xFFFF;
00287         char *name = cp[name_index].cp;
00288         char *signature = cp[signature_index].cp;
00289 
00290         if (type == CONSTANT_Fieldref) {
00291             if (! (is_legal_fieldname(cb, name, LegalField) &&
00292                is_legal_field_signature(cb, name, signature)))
00293                 return false;
00294         } else {
00295             if (! (is_legal_fieldname(cb, name, LegalMethod) &&
00296                is_legal_method_signature(cb, name, signature)))
00297                 return false;
00298         }
00299         break;
00300         }
00301     }
00302     }
00303     return true;
00304 }
00305 
00306 
00307 /*=========================================================================
00308  * FUNCTION:      is_legal_fieldname
00309  * OVERVIEW:      Returns true if the given name within the given ClassClass
00310  *                structure consists of a legal fieldname or a classname
00311  *                if the third argument is LegalClass.
00312  * INTERFACE:
00313  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00314  *                char*: legal field name or a classname
00315  *                int: type of name (class or field name)
00316  *
00317  *   returns:     boolean type
00318  *=======================================================================*/
00319 static bool
00320 is_legal_fieldname(ClassClass *cb, char *name, int type)
00321 {
00322     bool result;
00323     if (name[0] == '<') {
00324     result = (type == LegalMethod) &&
00325              ((strcmp(name, "<init>") == 0) ||
00326           (strcmp(name, "<clinit>") == 0));
00327     } else {
00328     char *p;
00329     if (type == LegalClass && name[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
00330         p = skip_over_field_signature(name, false);
00331     } else {
00332         p = skip_over_fieldname(name, type == LegalClass);
00333     }
00334     result = (p != 0 && p[0] == '\0');
00335     }
00336     if (!result) {
00337     char *thing =    (type == LegalField) ? "Field"
00338                    : (type == LegalMethod) ? "Method" : "Class";
00339 
00340     CCerror(cb, "Illegal %s name \"%s\"", thing, name);
00341     return false;
00342     } else {
00343         return true;
00344 
00345     }
00346 }
00347 
00348 /*=========================================================================
00349  * FUNCTION:      is_legal_field_signature
00350  * OVERVIEW:      Returns true if the entire given string within the given
00351  *                ClassClass structure consists of a legal field signature.
00352  * INTERFACE:
00353  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00354  *                char*: field name
00355  *                char*: field signature
00356  *
00357  *   returns:     boolean type
00358  *=======================================================================*/
00359 static bool
00360 is_legal_field_signature(ClassClass *cb, char *fieldname, char *signature)
00361 {
00362     char *p = skip_over_field_signature(signature, false);
00363     if (p != 0 && p[0] == '\0') {
00364     return true;
00365     } else {
00366     CCerror(cb, "Field \"%s\" has illegal signature \"%s\"",
00367            fieldname, signature);
00368     return false;
00369     }
00370 }
00371 
00372 /*=========================================================================
00373  * FUNCTION:      is_legal_method_signature
00374  * OVERVIEW:      Returns true if the entire given string within the given
00375  *                ClassClass structure consists of a legal method signature.
00376  * INTERFACE:
00377  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00378  *                char*: method name
00379  *                char*: method signature
00380  *
00381  *   returns:     boolean type
00382  *=======================================================================*/
00383 static bool
00384 is_legal_method_signature(ClassClass *cb, char *methodname, char *signature)
00385 {
00386     char *p = signature;
00387     char *next_p;
00388     /* The first character must be a '(' */
00389     if (*p++ == SIGNATURE_FUNC) {
00390     /* Skip over however many legal field signatures there are */
00391     while ((next_p = skip_over_field_signature(p, false)) != 0)
00392         p = next_p;
00393     /* The first non-signature thing better be a ')' */
00394     if (*p++ == SIGNATURE_ENDFUNC) {
00395         if (methodname[0] == '<') {
00396         /* All internal methods must return void */
00397         if ((p[0] == SIGNATURE_VOID) && (p[1] == '\0'))
00398             return true;
00399         } else {
00400         /* Now, we better just have a return value. */
00401         next_p =  skip_over_field_signature(p, true);
00402         if (next_p && next_p[0] == '\0')
00403             return true;
00404         }
00405     }
00406     }
00407     CCerror(cb, "Method \"%s\" has illegal signature \"%s\"",
00408        methodname, signature);
00409     return false;
00410 }
00411 
00412 
00413 /*=========================================================================
00414  * Automatic code generation tables
00415  *=======================================================================*/
00416   /* The following tables and code generated using: */
00417   /* java GenerateCharacter -verbose -c -identifiers -spec UnicodeData-2.1.2.txt -template check_class.c.template -o check_class.c 8 4 4 */
00418   /* The X table has 256 entries for a total of 256 bytes. */
00419 
00420   static unsigned char X[256] = {
00421       0,   1,   2,   3,   4,   5,   6,   7,  /* 0x0000 */
00422       7,   8,   9,  10,  11,  12,  13,  14,  /* 0x0800 */
00423      15,  16,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0x1000 */
00424       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  17,  18,  /* 0x1800 */
00425      19,  20,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0x2000 */
00426       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0x2800 */
00427      21,  22,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0x3000 */
00428       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0x3800 */
00429       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0x4000 */
00430       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  23,  23,  /* 0x4800 */
00431      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x5000 */
00432      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x5800 */
00433      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x6000 */
00434      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x6800 */
00435      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x7000 */
00436      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x7800 */
00437      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x8000 */
00438      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x8800 */
00439      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0x9000 */
00440      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  24,  /* 0x9800 */
00441       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0xA000 */
00442       7,   7,   7,   7,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0xA800 */
00443      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0xB000 */
00444      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0xB800 */
00445      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0xC000 */
00446      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  /* 0xC800 */
00447      23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  23,  25,  /* 0xD000 */
00448       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0xD800 */
00449       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0xE000 */
00450       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0xE800 */
00451       7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,   7,  /* 0xF000 */
00452       7,  23,  26,  27,  23,  28,  29,  30   /* 0xF800 */
00453   };
00454 
00455   /* The Y table has 496 entries for a total of 496 bytes. */
00456 
00457   static unsigned char Y[496] = {
00458       0,   0,   1,   2,   3,   4,   3,   5,  /*   0 */
00459       0,   0,   6,   7,   8,   9,   8,   9,  /*   0 */
00460       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  /*   1 */
00461       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  10,  /*   1 */
00462       8,  11,   0,   0,   0,   8,   8,   8,  /*   2 */
00463       8,   8,  12,  13,  14,  14,  15,   0,  /*   2 */
00464      16,  16,  16,  16,  17,   0,  18,  19,  /*   3 */
00465      20,   8,  21,   8,  22,  23,  24,  25,  /*   3 */
00466      26,   8,   8,   8,   8,  26,   8,   8,  /*   4 */
00467      27,   8,   8,   8,  28,   8,  29,  30,  /*   4 */
00468       0,   0,   0,   3,   8,  31,   3,   8,  /*   5 */
00469      11,  32,  33,  34,  35,   8,   5,  36,  /*   5 */
00470       0,   0,   3,   5,  37,  38,   2,  39,  /*   6 */
00471       8,   8,   8,  40,  22,  41,  42,   2,  /*   6 */
00472       0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*   7 */
00473       0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*   7 */
00474      43,   8,   8,  44,  45,  46,  47,   0,  /*   8 */
00475      48,  49,  50,  51,  52,  53,  47,  25,  /*   8 */
00476      54,  49,  50,  55,  56,  57,  58,  59,  /*   9 */
00477      60,  21,  50,  61,  62,   0,  63,   0,  /*   9 */
00478      48,  49,  50,  64,  65,  66,  67,   0,  /*  10 */
00479      68,  69,  70,  71,  72,  73,  74,   0,  /*  10 */
00480      75,  24,  50,  76,  77,  78,  67,   0,  /*  11 */
00481      79,  24,  50,  76,  77,  80,  67,   0,  /*  11 */
00482      79,  24,  50,  81,  82,  73,  67,   0,  /*  12 */
00483       0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  12 */
00484       3,   8,  22,  83,  84,   2,   0,   0,  /*  13 */
00485      85,  86,  87,  88,  89,  90,   0,   0,  /*  13 */
00486       0,  91,   2,  92,  93,   8,  94,  32,  /*  14 */
00487      95,  96,  45,  97,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  14 */
00488       0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  15 */
00489       0,   0,   8,   8,  98,   8,   8,  99,  /*  15 */
00490       8,   8,   8,   8,   8, 100,   8,   8,  /*  16 */
00491       8,   8, 101,   8,   8,   8,   8,  94,  /*  16 */
00492       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  /*  17 */
00493       8, 102,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  94,  /*  17 */
00494       8, 103,   8,   8, 103, 104,   8, 105,  /*  18 */
00495       8,   8,   8, 106, 107, 108, 109, 107,  /*  18 */
00496       0,   0,   0, 110, 111,   0,   0, 110,  /*  19 */
00497       0,   0, 109,   0,   0, 112, 113,   0,  /*  19 */
00498     114, 115, 116, 117,   0,   0,   8,   8,  /*  20 */
00499      36,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  20 */
00500     118,   0, 119, 120,   3,   8,   8,   8,  /*  21 */
00501       8, 121,   3,   8,   8,   8,   8, 122,  /*  21 */
00502     123,   8, 109,   3,   8,   8,   8,   8,  /*  22 */

00504       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  /*  23 */

00506       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  /*  24 */

00508       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,  /*  25 */
00509       8,   8,  25,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  25 */
00510       8,   8, 105,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  26 */
00511       0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,   0,  /*  26 */
00512      99, 124,  50, 125, 126,   8,   8,   8,  /*  27 */
00513       8,   8,   8,  14,   0, 127,   8,   8,  /*  27 */
00514       8,   8,   8, 105,   0,   8,   8,   8,  /*  28 */
00515       8, 128,   8,   8,  11,   0,   0, 102,  /*  28 */
00516       0,   0, 129, 130, 131,   0, 132, 133,  /*  29 */
00517       8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8,   8, 109,  /*  29 */
00518       1,   2,   3,   4,   3,   5, 134,   8,  /*  30 */
00519       8,   8,   8,  22, 135, 136, 137,   0   /*  30 */
00520   };
00521 
00522   /* The A table has 2208 entries for a total of 552 bytes. */
00523 
00524   static unsigned long A[138] = {
00525     0x00000000,  /*   0 */
00526     0x00000300,  /*   1 */
00527     0x00055555,  /*   2 */
00528     0xFFFFFFFC,  /*   3 */
00529     0xC03FFFFF,  /*   4 */
00530     0x003FFFFF,  /*   5 */
00531     0x00300FF0,  /*   6 */
00532     0x00300C00,  /*   7 */
00533     0xFFFFFFFF,  /*   8 */
00534     0xFFFF3FFF,  /*   9 */
00535     0xFFF00FFF,  /*  10 */
00536     0x0000FFFF,  /*  11 */
00537     0x0003FFFF,  /*  12 */
00538     0xFFC3FFFF,  /*  13 */
00539     0x0000000F,  /*  14 */
00540     0x000003FF,  /*  15 */
00541     0x55555555,  /*  16 */
00542     0x00000555,  /*  17 */
00543     0x00000005,  /*  18 */
00544     0x00300000,  /*  19 */
00545     0xF33F3000,  /*  20 */
00546     0xFFFFFFCF,  /*  21 */
00547     0x3FFFFFFF,  /*  22 */
00548     0x33303FFF,  /*  23 */
00549     0xFFFFFFF3,  /*  24 */
00550     0x000000FF,  /*  25 */
00551     0xF3FFFFFC,  /*  26 */
00552     0x0000154F,  /*  27 */
00553     0x03C3C3FF,  /*  28 */
00554     0xF0FFFFFF,  /*  29 */
00555     0x000F0FFF,  /*  30 */
00556     0x000C3FFF,  /*  31 */
00557     0x55555554,  /*  32 */
00558     0x55555545,  /*  33 */
00559     0x45455555,  /*  34 */
00560     0x00000114,  /*  35 */
00561     0x0000003F,  /*  36 */
00562     0x557FFFFF,  /*  37 */
00563     0x00000015,  /*  38 */

00565     0x3FF0FFFF,  /*  40 */

00567     0x05517D55,  /*  42 */
00568     0xFFFFFC54,  /*  43 */

00571     0xFFFF0154,  /*  46 */

00578     0xCF004000,  /*  53 */

00580     0x510F3CF3,  /*  55 */
00581     0x05414015,  /*  56 */
00582     0x33FC0000,  /*  57 */
00583     0x55555000,  /*  58 */
00584     0x000003F5,  /*  59 */

00598     0x00004000,  /*  73 */

00602     0x05515155,  /*  77 */

00614     0x055533FF,  /*  89 */
00615     0x0F055555,  /*  90 */
00616     0x00050000,  /*  91 */
00617     0x50044400,  /*  92 */
00618     0xFFFCFFFF,  /*  93 */
00619     0x000FFFFF,  /*  94 */
00620     0x00555155,  /*  95 */
00621     0x55544555,  /*  96 */
00622     0x00045554,  /*  97 */
00623     0x00000FFF,  /*  98 */
00624     0x00003FFF,  /*  99 */
00625     0xC00FFFFF,  /* 100 */
00626     0xFFFF003F,  /* 101 */
00627     0x00FFFFFF,  /* 102 */
00628     0x0FFF0FFF,  /* 103 */
00629     0xCCCCFFFF,  /* 104 */
00630     0x0FFFFFFF,  /* 105 */
00631     0x33FFF3FF,  /* 106 */
00632     0x03FFF3F0,  /* 107 */
00633     0x00FFF0FF,  /* 108 */
00634     0x03FFFFFF,  /* 109 */
00635     0xC0000000,  /* 110 */
00636     0x00000003,  /* 111 */
00637     0x01555555,  /* 112 */
00638     0x00000004,  /* 113 */
00639     0xFFF0C030,  /* 114 */
00640     0x0FFF0CFF,  /* 115 */
00641     0xFFF33300,  /* 116 */
00642     0x0003FFCF,  /* 117 */
00643     0x0000CC00,  /* 118 */
00644     0x555FFFFC,  /* 119 */
00645     0x00000FFC,  /* 120 */
00646     0x3FD403FF,  /* 121 */
00647     0x3F3FFFFF,  /* 122 */
00648     0xFFFFFC00,  /* 123 */
00649     0xD000FFC0,  /* 124 */
00650     0x33FF3FFF,  /* 125 */
00651     0xFFFFF3CF,  /* 126 */
00652     0xFFFFFFC0,  /* 127 */
00653     0xFFFFFFF0,  /* 128 */
00654     0x00000055,  /* 129 */
00655     0x000003C0,  /* 130 */
00656     0xFC000000,  /* 131 */
00657     0x000C0000,  /* 132 */
00658     0xFFFFF33F,  /* 133 */
00659     0xFFFFF000,  /* 134 */
00660     0xFFF0FFF0,  /* 135 */
00661     0x03F0FFF0,  /* 136 */
00662     0x00003C0F   /* 137 */
00663   };
00664 
00665   /* In all, the character property tables require 1304 bytes. */
00666 
00667 /*
00668  * This code mirrors Character.isJavaIdentifierStart.  It determines whether
00669  * the specified character is a legal start of a Java identifier as per JLS.
00670  *
00671  * The parameter ch is the character to be tested; return 1 if the
00672  * character is a letter, 0 otherwise.
00673  */
00674 #define isJavaIdentifierStart(ch) (((A[Y[(X[ch>>8]<<4)|((ch>>4)&0xF)]]>>((ch&0xF)<<1))&3) & 0x2)
00675 
00676 /*
00677  * This code mirrors Character.isJavaIdentifierPart.  It determines whether
00678  * the specified character is a legal part of a Java identifier as per JLS.
00679  *
00680  * The parameter ch is the character to be tested; return 1 if the
00681  * character is a digit, 0 otherwise.
00682  */
00683 #define isJavaIdentifierPart(ch) (((A[Y[(X[ch>>8]<<4)|((ch>>4)&0xF)]]>>((ch&0xF)<<1))&3) & 0x1)
00684 
00685 /*=========================================================================
00686  * FUNCTION:      skip_over_fieldname
00687  * OVERVIEW:      Skips over the longest part of the string that could be
00688  *                taken as a fieldname given a pointer to a string.
00689  *                Allows '/' if slash_okay parameter is true.
00690  *                Returns a pointer to just past the fieldname.
00691  *                Returns NULL if no fieldname is found, or in the case of
00692  *                slash_okay being TRUE, we may see consecutive slashes
00693  *                (meaning that we were looking for a qualified path, but
00694  *                found something that was badly-formed.)
00695  * INTERFACE:
00696  *   parameters:  char*: name
00697  *                boolean: slash_okay
00698  *
00699  *   returns:     char* field name or NULL
00700  *=======================================================================*/
00701 static char *
00702 skip_over_fieldname(char *name, bool slash_okay)
00703 {
00704     bool first;
00705     char *p;
00706     unicode last_ch = 0;
00707     for (p = name, first = true; ; first = false) {
00708     char *old_p = p;
00709     unicode ch = next_utf2unicode(&p);
00710     if (isJavaIdentifierStart(ch) || (!first && isJavaIdentifierPart(ch))
00711           || (slash_okay && ch == '/' && !first)
00712           || ch == '_' || ch == '$') {
00713         if (ch == '/' && last_ch == '/') {
00714         return 0;    /* Don't permit consecutive slashes */
00715         } else {
00716         last_ch = ch;
00717         }
00718     } else {
00719         return first ? 0 : old_p;
00720     }
00721     }
00722 }
00723 
00724 /*=========================================================================
00725  * FUNCTION:      skip_over_field_signature
00726  * OVERVIEW:      Skips over the longest part of the string that could be
00727  *                taken as a field signature given a pointer to a string.
00728  *                Allows "void" if void_okay parameter is true.
00729  *                Returns a pointer to just past the signature.
00730  *                Returns NULL if no legal signature is found.
00731  * INTERFACE:
00732  *   parameters:  char*: name
00733  *                boolean: void_okay
00734  *
00735  *   returns:     char* field signature or NULL
00736  *=======================================================================*/
00737 static char *
00738 skip_over_field_signature(char *name, bool void_okay)
00739 {
00740     for (;;) {
00741     switch (name[0]) {
00742             case SIGNATURE_VOID:
00743         if (!void_okay) return 0;
00744         /* FALL THROUGH */
00745             case SIGNATURE_BOOLEAN:
00746             case SIGNATURE_BYTE:
00747             case SIGNATURE_CHAR:
00748             case SIGNATURE_SHORT:
00749             case SIGNATURE_INT:
00750             case SIGNATURE_LONG:
00751         return name + 1;
00752 
00753             case SIGNATURE_FLOAT:
00754             case SIGNATURE_DOUBLE:
00755                 return no_floating_point ? NULL : name + 1;
00756 
00757         case SIGNATURE_CLASS: {
00758         /* Skip over the classname, if one is there. */
00759         char *p = skip_over_fieldname(name + 1, true);
00760         /* The next character better be a semicolon. */
00761         if (p && p[0] == ';') {
00762             return p + 1;
00763                 }
00764         return NULL;
00765         }
00766 
00767         case SIGNATURE_ARRAY:
00768         /* The rest of what's there better be a legal signature.  */
00769         name++;
00770         void_okay = false;
00771         break;
00772 
00773         default:
00774         return NULL;
00775     }
00776     }
00777 }
00778 
00779 /*=========================================================================
00780  * FUNCTION:      CCerror
00781  * OVERVIEW:      Handles formating errors found during class file
00782  *                verification.
00783  * INTERFACE:
00784  *   parameters:  pointer to the ClassClass structure.
00785  *                char *: format
00786  *
00787  *   returns:     nothing
00788  *=======================================================================*/
00789 static void
00790 CCerror (ClassClass *cb, char *format, ...)
00791 {
00792     if (verbose) {
00793     va_list args;
00794     jio_fprintf(stderr, "VERIFIER CLASS ERROR %s:\n", cbName(cb));
00795     va_start(args, format);
00796         jio_vfprintf(stderr, format, args);
00797     va_end(args);
00798     jio_fprintf(stderr, "\n");
00799     }
00800 }
00801 
00802 /*=========================================================================
00803  * FUNCTION:      IsLegalClassname
00804  * OVERVIEW:      Determines if the specified name is a legal UTF name for
00805  *                a class name.
00806  *                Note that this routine is intended for external use and
00807  *                expects the internal form of qualified classes
00808  *                (i.e. the dots should have been replaced by slashes.)
00809  * INTERFACE:
00810  *   parameters:  char*: name
00811  *                boolean: allowArrayClass
00812  *
00813  *   returns:     boolean type
00814  *=======================================================================*/
00815 bool IsLegalClassname(char *name, bool allowArrayClass)
00816 {
00817     char *p;
00818     if (name[0] == SIGNATURE_ARRAY) {
00819     if (!allowArrayClass) {
00820         return false;
00821     } else {
00822         /* Everything that's left better be a field signature */
00823         p = skip_over_field_signature(name, false);
00824     }
00825     } else {
00826     /* skip over the fieldname.  Slashes are okay */
00827     p = skip_over_fieldname(name, true);
00828     }
00829     return (p != 0 && p[0] == '\0');
00830 }
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:27 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
